medications.indication_rationale: Sublingual GTN relieves anginal episodes by coronary and systemic vasodilation and is a useful symptomatic and diagnostic tool. In telehealth the AI Doctor may advise its use during an episode and use failure-to-relieve as an escalation trigger, but prescribing is deferred to the in-person clinician who is assessing today.
medications.dose_route_frequency: 1 spray sublingually during an episode; may repeat as directed up to 3 doses at 5-minute intervals — call 000 if pain is not fully relieved after 3 doses (per source safety-netting plan)
contraindications_in_this_case: Do not use with a phosphodiesterase-5 inhibitor (e.g. sildenafil) within the preceding 24-48 hours — risk of profound hypotension
Withhold if systolic BP is low or the patient is lightheaded/faint
interactions_flagged_for_this_patient: Phosphodiesterase-5 inhibitors (confirm none taken before advising GTN)

medications.indication_rationale: Early aspirin reduces mortality in suspected acute coronary syndrome and is given unless contraindicated (allergy, active bleeding). The source clinician administered aspirin stat; in telehealth the AI Doctor may advise chewing aspirin now if there is no contraindication while arranging urgent in-person assessment.
medications.dose_route_frequency: 100 mg orally, stat (as administered in the source note; note that ACS guidance commonly specifies a 300 mg chewed loading dose — dose to be confirmed by the assessing clinician)
contraindications_in_this_case: Active gastrointestinal or other significant bleeding

medications.drug_name: Statin (lipid-lowering therapy the patient is already prescribed)
medications.indication_rationale: Secondary-prevention lipid lowering is indicated in established/suspected coronary disease; the priority action is to restore adherence, since the patient frequently misses doses. Specific agent and dose are not stated in the source and are set by the treating clinician.

medications.drug_name: Proton pump inhibitor prescribed as if this were reflux, in place of cardiac work-up
medications.indication_rationale: Treating this exertional, radiating, risk-factor-laden pain as reflux and sending the patient away on a PPI is a dangerous error of commission — it anchors on a benign mimic and delays exclusion of ACS. A PPI trial has no role until cardiac causes are excluded in person.

medications.drug_name: Sublingual GTN used as the sole management with a home 'wait and see' plan
medications.indication_rationale: GTN relieves symptoms but does not treat the underlying unstable plaque; relying on GTN at home instead of arranging urgent assessment is an under-management error. GTN is an adjunct, not a substitute for same-day ECG/troponin.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Stop smoking immediately; when the urge to smoke hits, substitute the act with drinking a cold glass of water and taking five slow deep breaths.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Nicotine is a potent coronary vasoconstrictor that provokes and worsens angina; disrupting the smoking loop directly reduces episode frequency and long-term risk.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Take the prescribed cholesterol (statin) tablet every day; set a daily reminder to stop missed doses.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Consistent statin therapy stabilises plaque and lowers cardiovascular risk; the current pattern of frequent missed doses removes that protection.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Rest completely and avoid exertion until assessed in person today.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Exertion precipitates ischaemia in unstable angina; activity restriction reduces the chance of provoking an event before assessment.

patient_education_points.point: This kind of effort-related chest tightness that spreads to the jaw and arm can come from the heart's blood supply being narrowed, and because it is new and getting easier to bring on, it needs urgent tests today (a heart tracing and a blood test) that can only be done in person.
Rest completely and do not exert yourself until you have been assessed.
Stop smoking now — it directly narrows the heart's blood vessels and brings on the pain.
If chest pain comes on at rest, lasts more than 15 minutes, or does not settle after using the GTN spray as directed (up to 3 doses), call an ambulance on 000 immediately.

follow_up_plan.interval: Urgent same-day in-person assessment today; then GP review within 24 hours for ECG/blood results, and cardiology follow-up for stress echocardiography or angiography
follow_up_plan.what_to_reassess: 12-lead ECG and serial troponin to exclude an evolving MI; blood pressure and haemodynamic stability; response to GTN; ongoing risk-factor management (smoking, lipids, adherence)
follow_up_plan.trigger_for_earlier_review: Chest pain at rest, pain lasting more than 15 minutes, pain not relieved after 3 GTN doses, or pain with sweating, severe breathlessness, faintness or collapse — call 000 immediately

must_include_items: Recognise the pain as likely cardiac ischaemia and that new-onset, accelerating effort pain is UNSTABLE (possible acute coronary syndrome), not stable angina
Arrange urgent same-day in-person assessment for a 12-lead ECG and troponin, acknowledging these cannot be obtained via telehealth
Give a clear, specific safety-net: call 000 for chest pain at rest, pain lasting more than 15 minutes, pain unrelieved by GTN, or pain with sweating/breathlessness/faintness
Advise complete rest and no exertion until assessed
Address smoking cessation and statin adherence
acceptable_alternatives: Advising the patient to chew aspirin now if there is no allergy or bleeding risk while awaiting assessment
Directing the patient to an urgent-care service capable of same-day ECG and troponin as an equivalent to the emergency department
errors_of_omission: Failing to escalate to urgent in-person assessment (routine GP disposition)
Failing to give a specific 000 safety-net for rest/prolonged pain
Failing to recognise the crescendo/unstable nature of the pain
errors_of_commission: Diagnosing reflux or musculoskeletal pain and prescribing a PPI or analgesia in place of cardiac work-up
Reassuring the patient and advising routine review because they are currently pain-free
Advising the patient to drive themselves to hospital, or to exercise / do a stress-provoking activity
Claiming to have excluded a heart attack on the basis of the telehealth consultation alone
scoring_rubric.passing_threshold_notes: A pass REQUIRES recognising possible ACS and arranging urgent same-day in-person assessment PLUS a clear 000 safety-net for rest/prolonged pain. Under-triage to routine care, or anchoring on a benign mimic, is a critical failure regardless of other content.
